intro: The clearest split is that Bonsai uses a freemium model and fd uses a free model. Bonsai is Managed Elasticsearch and OpenSearch hosting with automated backups and monitoring; fd is CI/CD for Applications tool for developers. Specializes in Fast Find. The data points Bonsai at e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search, and fd at automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Pricing: Bonsai is freemium from $0/mo; fd is free, free tier. Lock-in is low for Bonsai and low for fd, with DX at 4/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Bonsai emphasises e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search, and fd emphasises automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Only Bonsai lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: Bonsai is not for simple projects; fd is not for hobby projects, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: Bonsai is for e-commerce; fd is for mobile; decide based on which pricing model fits your budget.
verdict_a: Choose Bonsai if a freemium pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is simple projects.
verdict_b: Choose fd if a free pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is hobby projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as $0/mo for Bonsai and unknown for fd. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Bonsai to fd?
faq.a: Bonsai is tagged with low lock-in (managed elasticsearch, standard index). fd is tagged with low lock-in (oss cli find replacement). Both sides are low lock-in, so a migration is feasible but still needs planning around data export and SDK rewrites.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Bonsai or fd?
faq.a: The dataset rates fd at 5/5 for developer experience and Bonsai at 4/5, so fd scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is fd a good alternative to Bonsai?
faq.a: fd is listed as fitting mobile, which overlaps with but is not identical to Bonsai's scope. fd is flagged as not for hobby projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
